title: AI boom puts SK Hynix on the cusp of $1 trillion market value
summary: South Korea is at the forefront of Asia's AI boom, with SK Hynix nearing a $1 trillion market value, following Samsung Electronics' recent milestone. Driven by surging AI demand for memory chips, SK Hynix shares have seen significant growth.

title: Cisco to cut about 4,000 jobs in AI-focused restructuring as orders surge
summary: Cisco announced nearly 4,000 job cuts as part of a strategic restructuring to boost investment in AI and growth areas. The networking giant also raised its annual revenue forecast, driven by a significant surge in hyperscaler orders for AI infrastructure, leading to a substantial stock increase.

title: Anthropic overtakes OpenAI in enterprise artificial intelligence race
summary: New data reveals Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI in enterprise AI spending, with 34.4% of surveyed companies paying for its products in April. This shift is largely driven by demand for Anthropic's AI coding assistant, Claude Code, marking a significant disruption in the market.

title: TSMC says global chip market to hit $1.5 trillion by 2030 as AI drives growth
summary: TSMC predicts the global chip market will surpass $1.5 trillion by 2030. AI and high-performance computing will lead this growth. The company is expanding its advanced chip production and packaging capacity. New facilities are being built in Arizona, Japan, and Germany to meet future demand.

title: Nvidia CEO hopes Trump and Xi will improve two-way ties: CCTV
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang met with Chinese President Xi Jinping. He expressed hope for improved US-China relations. Nvidia faces difficulties supplying advanced AI chips to Chinese clients. This impacts their artificial intelligence development efforts.

title: Samsung Electronics urges union to resume talks as strike threat looms
summary: Samsung's labour union has rejected a call to resume pay talks, demanding transparency in the company's bonus scheme. A threatened 18-day strike looms if demands aren't met, with the government urging both sides to negotiate to protect South Korea's vital chip export economy.
